title: [JSInvokable] attribute should only be used on public methods
description: [JSInvokable] attribute should only be used on public methodsAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugblazorWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoIn Blazor, the[JSInvokable]attribute is used to annotate a method, enabling it to be invoked from JavaScript code. The prerequisite for this functionality is that the method must be declared aspublic.Otherwise, a runtime error will be triggered when an attempt is made to call the method from JavaScript.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects [JSInvokable] attribute used on non-public methods in Blazor components
logic.triggering_conditions: (Attribute is JSInvokable) AND (Following method is private/internal/protected)
logic.reasoning: The [JSInvokable] attribute in Blazor enables JavaScript interop by making C# methods callable from JavaScript. This attribute requires the method to be public, otherwise a runtime error occurs when JavaScript attempts to invoke it. Detecting non-public methods with this attribute prevents runtime failures.
